introduction:
A Death Certificate in Maharashtra is an official document issued by the local Municipal Corporation, Municipal Council, or Gram Panchayat under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, 1969. It serves as legal proof of the death of an individual and records essential details such as the name, date, place, and cause of death.

The death certificate is mandatory for claiming life insurance, settling bank accounts, transferring property, obtaining succession certificates, and settling pension/gratuity claims. In Maharashtra, death registration is managed through the Civil Registration System (CRS) and the Aaple Sarkar portal.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Death must be registered within 21 days of occurrence (free of charge)
  • Registration after 21 days but within 30 days requires late fee
  • Registration after 1 year requires Magistrate's order
  • The first copy of the death certificate is FREE; duplicate copies cost ₹10–₹50
  • The informant (close relative, hospital, or person present at death) must report the death
  • Track your application at crsorgi.gov.in or aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Death Certificate

Note: Carry original documents for verification and submit self-attested photocopies with the application.
Proof of Death
  • Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD) from hospital
  • Hospital discharge summary (if death occurred in hospital)
  • Cremation / Burial ground receipt
  • Panchnama by police (in case of unnatural death)
Identity Proof of Deceased
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • Ration Card
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar Card of the informant (person reporting)
  • Proof of relationship with deceased
  • Address proof of informant
  • Passport-size photograph of informant
For Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit on ₹100 stamp paper (after 30 days)
  • Magistrate's order (after 1 year)
  • Non-availability certificate from Registrar
  • Any supporting proof of death (hospital records, newspaper cutting)

Note: Late fee applies for registration beyond 21 days.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Register Death Online via CRS / Aaple Sarkar

Maharashtra allows online death registration through the CRS portal and the Aaple Sarkar platform. You need a mobile number for OTP verification.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to crsorgi.gov.in or aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Click "Sign Up" and create an account using your mobile number. Verify via OTP and log in.
  3. Select "Death Registration"
    Navigate to Public Health → Birth & Death → Register Death.
  4. Fill the Death Registration Form
    Enter details accurately:
    • Full name of deceased, gender, age, date of death
    • Place of death (hospital / residence / other)
    • Cause of death (as per MCCD)
    • Informant details and relationship
    • Address of the deceased
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of MCCD, ID proof, and informant documents. File size limit: 200 KB per file (JPG/PDF)
  6. Submit & Note Reference Number
    Click Submit. Note down the Application Reference Number sent via SMS. Save this number!
  7. Verification & Approval
    The local Registrar verifies the details within 7–15 working days.
Tip: Once approved, download the Death Certificate directly from the CRS portal using your reference number.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Death Offline at Municipal / Gram Panchayat Office

You can visit the local Municipal Corporation, Nagar Palika, or Gram Panchayat office to register the death in person.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Nearest Registration Office
    Go to your local Municipal Corporation / Nagar Palika / Gram Panchayat office. Working hours: Monday – Saturday, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
  2. Collect Death Registration Form
    Ask for the Death Registration Form (Form No. 2) from the counter. The form is free of cost.
  3. Fill the Form Carefully
    Use black ink and BLOCK LETTERS. Enter:
    • Deceased person's full name, date of birth, gender
    • Date, time, and place of death
    • Cause of death
    • Informant's name, address, and relationship
    • Father's / husband's name of deceased
  4. Attach Required Documents
    Submit self-attested photocopies of MCCD, deceased's ID proof, and informant's ID proof. Carry originals for verification.
  5. Submit & Get Acknowledgement
    Submit the form at the counter. You will receive an Acknowledgement Receipt with your registration number.
  6. Collect Death Certificate
    After verification, collect the death certificate from the same office within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days if registered within 21 days. Delayed registrations may take longer.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit crsorgi.gov.in or Aaple Sarkar
Enter your Reference Number to check the current status.

Download Death Certificate

Once approved, download the digitally signed certificate from the CRS portal. The digital copy is valid for all legal and official purposes.

Use for Legal Proceedings

Use the certificate for insurance claims, property transfer, bank account settlement, pension/gratuity claims, and succession certificate applications.

Raise a Grievance

If not processed within 15 days, call Toll-Free: 1800-233-4567 or visit Aaple Sarkar to file an online grievance.
